frameworks.name: Neural Engineering Framework
frameworks.one_line: constructive prescription that jointly determines tuning curves and recurrent weights from a chosen low-dim trajectory

frameworks.name: attractor dynamics
frameworks.one_line: ring attractor for orientation tuning derived as a canonical NEF instance

frameworks.name: low-dimensional population coding
frameworks.one_line: empirical observation that neural population dimensionality is much lower than population size

core_question: How can a chosen low-dimensional dynamical system (a ring) be mapped constructively onto high-dimensional neural population activity?
key_assumptions: neural population activity lies on a low-dimensional manifold
a target low-dim trajectory and a neural basis can be chosen a priori
tuning curves and recurrent weights are jointly determined by these choices
manipulations: choice of neural basis for the population code
key_findings: ring attractor for orientation tuning can be derived from NEF
tuning curves and recurrent weight matrix are jointly determined by the chosen low-dim trajectory and neural basis
the derivation gives a constructive prescription for high-dim to low-dim mappings
interpretations: mapping low-dim dynamics to high-dim neural space is a common feature of cortical computation
the ring attractor literature and the NEF tradition are formally related through this construction
